{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Almond Technologies Limited is a Renewable Energy &amp; power Solution Company that specializes in the provision of environmental-friendly (green energy) supported &amp; back up by the national grid and Generator to guaranteed uninterrupted power supply service to our valued customers.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive&nbsp;

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Business Development Executive to drive&nbsp;&nbsp;sales, identify new business opportunities, and expand our customer base for renewable energy solutions.
	The ideal candidate must have a strong sales background, excellent negotiation skills, and a proven track record of meeting revenue targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Generate new business leads and convert them into sales.
	Promote and sell the company&rsquo;s renewable energy products and services.
	Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients.
	Prepare and deliver sales presentations, proposals, and quotations.
	Negotiate contracts and close deals.
	Meet and exceed monthly and annual sales targets.
	Conduct market research to identify new business opportunities.
	Follow up on customer enquiries and ensure excellent after-sales service.
	Collaborate with the technical team to deliver solutions that meet client needs.
	Prepare regular sales and business development reports.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, Engineering, or a related field.


Experience:


	5&ndash;10 years of proven experience in sales and business development.
	Experience in the renewable energy, solar, electrical, engineering, or related industry is highly preferred.
	Demonstrated track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.


Skills:


	Strong sales and business development experience.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
	Proven ability to close high-value sales.
	Customer relationship management skills.
	Strong networking and prospecting abilities.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint).


Other Requirements:


	Must be highly target-driven and self-motivated.
	Strong understanding of B2B sales and client relationship management.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work with minimal supervision.
	Willingness to travel for client meetings and business development activities.
	A valid driver&rsquo;s licence is an added advantage.


Remuneration
N350,000 per month, plus performance-based incentives and commissions.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: info@almondtechnologies.com using &ldquo;Business Development Executive Application&rdquo; as the subject of mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Business Development Executive Job at Almond Technologies Limited




        
    

        
        Almond Technologies Limited is a Renewable Energy & power Solution Company that specializes in the provision of environmental-friendly (green energy) supported & back up by the national grid and Generator to guaranteed uninterrupted power supply service to our valued customers.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive 

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Business Development Executive to drive  sales, identify new business opportunities, and expand our customer base for renewable energy solutions.
	The ideal candidate must have a strong sales background, excellent negotiation skills, and a proven track record of meeting revenue targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Generate new business leads and convert them into sales.
	Promote and sell the company’s renewable energy products and services.
	Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients.
	Prepare and deliver sales presentations, proposals, and quotations.
	Negotiate contracts and close deals.
	Meet and exceed monthly and annual sales targets.
	Conduct market research to identify new business opportunities.
	Follow up on customer enquiries and ensure excellent after-sales service.
	Collaborate with the technical team to deliver solutions that meet client needs.
	Prepare regular sales and business development reports.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, Engineering, or a related field.


Experience:


	5–10 years of proven experience in sales and business development.
	Experience in the renewable energy, solar, electrical, engineering, or related industry is highly preferred.
	Demonstrated track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.


Skills:


	Strong sales and business development experience.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
	Proven ability to close high-value sales.
	Customer relationship management skills.
	Strong networking and prospecting abilities.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint).


Other Requirements:


	Must be highly target-driven and self-motivated.
	Strong understanding of B2B sales and client relationship management.
	Ability to  work with minimal supervision.
	Willingness to travel for client meetings and business development activities.
	A valid driver’s licence is an added advantage.


Remuneration
N350,000 per month, plus performance-based incentives and commissions.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Business Development Executive Application” as the subject of mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cashier",
  "description": "Swift Consulting Limited is a professional services firm specializing in Education Consulting, Human Resource Management, and Management Consulting. We connect top talent with leading organizations across industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Cashier&nbsp;

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a trustworthy, detail-oriented, and organized Cashier to join our cooperative society.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for handling daily cash transactions, processing loan repayments and disbursements, maintaining accurate financial records, and providing excellent customer service to members while ensuring compliance with the cooperative&#39;s financial policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and process loan repayments, savings deposits, and other member payments.
	Disburse approved loans to members in line with established procedures.
	Issue receipts for all transactions and maintain proper documentation.
	Balance the cash drawer and reconcile daily cash transactions.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly cash reports.
	Maintain accurate records of all cash inflows and outflows.
	Deposit cash into designated bank accounts when required.
	Ensure the security and confidentiality of cash and financial records.
	Respond professionally to members&#39; inquiries regarding payments and transactions.
	Work closely with the Accounts and Loan Officers to ensure accurate financial records.
	Report any cash discrepancies or suspicious transactions immediately.
	Perform other duties as assigned by management.


Requirements


	OND in Accounting, Banking &amp; Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience as a Cashier, Teller, or in a similar financial role.
	Experience in a microfinance bank, cooperative society, or financial institution is an added advantage.
	Good knowledge of cash handling procedures and basic accounting principles.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel.
	Excellent numerical, organizational, and record-keeping skills.
	Strong integrity, honesty, and attention to detail.
	Good communication and customer service skills.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work under pressure with minimal supervision.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hireme@swiftconsulting.com.ng using &quot;Cashier&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Cashier Job at Swift Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Swift Consulting Limited is a professional services firm specializing in Education Consulting, Human Resource Management, and Management Consulting. We connect top talent with leading organizations across industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Cashier 

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a trustworthy, detail-oriented, and organized Cashier to join our cooperative society.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for handling daily cash transactions, processing loan repayments and disbursements, maintaining accurate financial records, and providing excellent customer service to members while ensuring compliance with the cooperative's financial policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and process loan repayments, savings deposits, and other member payments.
	Disburse approved loans to members in line with established procedures.
	Issue receipts for all transactions and maintain proper documentation.
	Balance the cash drawer and reconcile daily cash transactions.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly cash reports.
	Maintain accurate records of all cash inflows and outflows.
	Deposit cash into designated bank accounts when required.
	Ensure the security and confidentiality of cash and financial records.
	Respond professionally to members' inquiries regarding payments and transactions.
	Work closely with the Accounts and Loan Officers to ensure accurate financial records.
	Report any cash discrepancies or suspicious transactions immediately.
	Perform other duties as assigned by management.


Requirements


	OND in Accounting, Banking & Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	1–2 years of experience as a Cashier, Teller, or in a similar financial role.
	Experience in a microfinance bank, cooperative society, or financial institution is an added advantage.
	Good knowledge of cash handling procedures and basic accounting principles.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel.
	Excellent numerical, organizational, and record-keeping skills.
	Strong integrity, honesty, and attention to detail.
	Good communication and customer service skills.
	Ability to  work under pressure with minimal supervision.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Cashier" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Control Officer",
  "description": "Bere-Mont Nigeria Limited, an Educational firm, is&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the following positions below:&nbsp;

&nbsp;Job Position: Internal Control Officer

Job Location: Rivers
Employment Type: Full-Time
Department: Finance &amp; Operations
Gender: Male (For gender balance)
Industry: Education

Description


	We are seeking a highly experienced, detail-oriented, and results-driven Internal Control Officer to strengthen our governance, risk management, and internal control framework in our school.
	The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring the effectiveness of internal controls, safeguarding organizational assets, promoting compliance with policies and regulations, and supporting operational excellence across the organization.
	This is a strategic position suited for a seasoned professional with strong expertise in internal controls, audit, risk management, compliance, financial oversight, and process improvement.
	The Internal Controller will&nbsp;&nbsp;work closely with management and departmental leaders to identify risks, enforce accountability, and drive continuous improvement in operational and financial processes.


Key Responsibilities
Internal Control &amp; Compliance:


	Develop, implement, and maintain a robust internal control framework across all business functions.
	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls, policies, and procedures.
	Ensure adherence to organizational policies, statutory regulations, and industry best practices.
	Monitor compliance with financial, operational, and regulatory requirements.
	Recommend corrective actions and monitor implementation of agreed management actions.


Risk Management:


	Identify, assess, and evaluate operational, financial, compliance, and strategic risks.
	Develop risk mitigation strategies and ensure effective monitoring of risk exposures.
	Conduct periodic risk assessments and provide recommendations to line manager.
	Maintain an enterprise-wide risk register and monitor emerging risks.


Financial Controls &amp; Oversight:


	Review financial transactions to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with established procedures.
	Monitor budgeting and expenditure controls across departments.
	Review procurement, inventory, and asset management processes to identify and control weaknesses.


Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Engagement:


	Prepare periodic reports on internal controls, risk assessments, audit findings, and compliance status.
	Present findings and recommendations to line manager.
	Provide advisory support to departments on control-related matters and best practices.


Qualifications and Experience
Education:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certifications (ACA, ICAN,ACCA, CIA,CFA) and risk management certifications will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in Internal Control, Internal Audit, Risk Management, Compliance, or Finance.
	Proven experience in designing and implementing internal control frameworks.
	Experience working in a large corporate organization, educational institution, financial institution, or similar complex environment.
	Demonstrated success in identifying and mitigating business risks.
	Strong analytical and investigative skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, ERP systems, and data analysis tools.:
	Must be between 30 -45 years of age.


Behavioral Competencies


	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.
	Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.
	Excellent communication and report-writing abilities.
	Exceptional attention to detail and problem-solving capabilities.
	Ability to work independently and make sound judgments.
	Strong organizational and project management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Applications to: riversschools@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;

Internal Control Officer Job at Bere-Mont Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Bere-Mont Nigeria Limited, an Educational firm, is  recruiting to fill the following positions below: 

 Job Position: Internal Control Officer

Job Location: Rivers
Employment Type: Full-Time
Department: Finance & Operations
Gender: Male (For gender balance)
Industry: Education

Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced, detail-oriented, and results-driven Internal Control Officer to strengthen our governance, risk management, and internal control framework in our school.
	The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring the effectiveness of internal controls, safeguarding organizational assets, promoting compliance with policies and regulations, and supporting operational excellence across the organization.
	This is a strategic position suited for a seasoned professional with strong expertise in internal controls, audit, risk management, compliance, financial oversight, and process improvement.
	The Internal Controller will  work closely with management and departmental leaders to identify risks, enforce accountability, and drive continuous improvement in operational and financial processes.


Key Responsibilities
Internal Control & Compliance:


	Develop, implement, and maintain a robust internal control framework across all business functions.
	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls, policies, and procedures.
	Ensure adherence to organizational policies, statutory regulations, and industry best practices.
	Monitor compliance with financial, operational, and regulatory requirements.
	Recommend corrective actions and monitor implementation of agreed management actions.


Risk Management:


	Identify, assess, and evaluate operational, financial, compliance, and strategic risks.
	Develop risk mitigation strategies and ensure effective monitoring of risk exposures.
	Conduct periodic risk assessments and provide recommendations to line manager.
	Maintain an enterprise-wide risk register and monitor emerging risks.


Financial Controls & Oversight:


	Review financial transactions to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with established procedures.
	Monitor budgeting and expenditure controls across departments.
	Review procurement, inventory, and asset management processes to identify and control weaknesses.


Reporting & Stakeholder Engagement:


	Prepare periodic reports on internal controls, risk assessments, audit findings, and compliance status.
	Present findings and recommendations to line manager.
	Provide advisory support to departments on control-related matters and best practices.


Qualifications and Experience
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certifications (ACA, ICAN,ACCA, CIA,CFA) and risk management certifications will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in Internal Control, Internal Audit, Risk Management, Compliance, or Finance.
	Proven experience in designing and implementing internal control frameworks.
	Experience working in a large corporate organization, educational institution, financial institution, or similar complex environment.
	Demonstrated success in identifying and mitigating business risks.
	Strong analytical and investigative skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, ERP systems, and data analysis tools.:
	Must be between 30 -45 years of age.


Behavioral Competencies


	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.
	Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.
	Excellent communication and report-writing abilities.
	Exceptional attention to detail and problem-solving capabilities.
	Ability to work independently and make sound judgments.
	Strong organizational and project management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail. 

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

SME Loan Officer Job at Dash Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Dash Microfinance Bank Limited is a fully licensed financial institution established to deliver credit-led, technology-powered banking services to underserved individuals and small businesses in Nigeria. 

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: SME Loan Officer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria

Requirements
     

            
    




	Bachelor's degree in Banking & Finance, Business Administration, Economics, Accounting, Marketing, or related fields.
	Minimum 3 years of post-NYSC experience in SME lending or relationship management within a bank, MFB, or other financial institution.
	Strong sales, business development, and credit analysis skills.
	Excellent relationship management and communication skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

	At least 2 years in a supervisory role.
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, CIA, ACAMS, or CFE) an advantage.

Audit & Compliance Lead Job at Dash Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Dash Microfinance Bank Limited is a fully licensed financial institution established to deliver credit-led, technology-powered banking services to underserved individuals and small businesses in Nigeria. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Audit & Compliance Lead

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria

Requirements
     

            
    




	Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of post-NYSC experience in internal audit, compliance, or internal control (banking/microfinance banking).
	At least 2 years in a supervisory role.
	Professional certification (ICAN, ACCA, CIA, ACAMS, or CFE) an advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using as the Job Position as subject of your email.
